evals.description: Valid fix — all five gates pass

triage.reasoning: PHP fatal error in plugin file

suspected_causes.evidence: Error file path names the plugin directly

diagnostic.primary_diagnosis: PHP fatal error in broken-plugin on line 42.

fix.fix_title: Deactivate broken-plugin causing PHP fatal error
fix.root_cause_summary: PHP fatal error in /wp-content/plugins/broken-plugin/broken-plugin.php on line 42 — the plugin calls some_deprecated_function() which no longer exists.

fix_steps.action: Go to Plugins → Installed Plugins and deactivate 'Broken Plugin'
fix_steps.expected_result: White screen resolves and admin loads

fix_steps.action: If wp-admin is inaccessible, run: wp plugin deactivate broken-plugin

verification_steps: Clear browser cache and reload the homepage
Confirm the white screen is gone and the homepage renders correctly
fix.if_this_does_not_work: Enable WP_DEBUG by adding define('WP_DEBUG', true); define('WP_DEBUG_LOG', true); to wp-config.php, reload the page, and check the contents of wp-content/debug.log for additional errors

input.original_error_text: Fatal error: Call to undefined function some_deprecated_function() in /wp-content/plugins/broken-plugin/broken-plugin.php on line 42

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
confidence_in_fix is 'high' or 'medium' (error text is clear and fix is specific)

evals.description: Fix fails specificity gate — plugin name missing from action

suspected_causes.evidence: Error file names ACF Pro directly

diagnostic.primary_diagnosis: ACF Pro redeclaration error on line 301.

input.original_error_text: Fatal error: Cannot redeclare acf_register_block_type() in /wp-content/plugins/acf-pro/acf.php on line 301

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
At least one rejection reason has gate: 'specificity'
The specificity rejection reason cites Step 1 or the action field lacking a plugin name
The suggestion in the rejection tells the generator to name 'ACF Pro' specifically

evals.description: Fix fails safety gate — wp-config.php edit marked safe_to_do_on_live true with no rollback

suspected_causes.evidence: Redirect loop after migration to HTTPS

diagnostic.primary_diagnosis: Redirect loop likely caused by force_ssl_admin setting.

fix.fix_title: Fix redirect loop by editing wp-config.php
fix.root_cause_summary: HTTPS force redirect in wp-config.php causing loop.

fix_steps.action: Edit wp-config.php and remove the line: define('FORCE_SSL_ADMIN', true);

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
At least one rejection reason has gate: 'safety'
The safety rejection cites Step 1 editing wp-config.php being marked safe_to_do_on_live: true without a rollback_instruction
The completeness gate may also fail — 'Contact your hosting provider' is too generic for if_this_does_not_work
